questions.questionText: Magnesium sulfate in eclampsia is primarily used to:
questions.optionD: Improve placental perfusion by vasodilation alone
questions.explanation: Magnesium sulfate is the drug of choice for prevention and treatment of eclamptic seizures. It is not the primary antihypertensive agent. It does not induce labor. Although it has vascular effects, its key clinical role is anticonvulsant prophylaxis and treatment.

questions.questionText: A pregnant woman at 34 weeks has hypertension, proteinuria, headache, and visual symptoms. The definitive treatment is:
questions.explanation: In severe preeclampsia with warning symptoms, definitive treatment is delivery after maternal stabilization, balancing maternal and fetal status. Bed rest and methyldopa alone are not definitive. Diuretics are not routine primary therapy unless specific indications exist such as pulmonary edema.

questions.questionText: HELLP syndrome is characterized by all except:
questions.explanation: HELLP stands for hemolysis, elevated liver enzymes, and low platelets. Hypercalcemia is not part of the syndrome. Recognizing HELLP is crucial because it indicates severe disease with high maternal-fetal risk.
questions.source: Jeffcoate’s Principles of Gynaecology and Obstetrics, Medical Disorders in Pregnancy
